events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to assess block/brick laying work on a residential job, specifically head joints unfilled (visible voids). The work stage is rough-complete. Reference: TMS 402/602 (The Masonry Society's code for masonry structures). Unfilled head joints are a defect; it compromises structural integrity, weather resistance, fire resistance, etc. For structural masonry, TMS 402 requires full bedding of mortar in all joints. Head joints must be filled solidly. Visible voids mean non-compliance. So decision = fail. Risk: could be high or critical depending on load, but likely medium to high for residential. I'll assign "high" because voids affect strength. Work stage: rough-complete as given. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant (both apply). Percent complete: maybe 70-80% since rough-complete but not finished. I'll guess 80%. Value: 0 (no numeric reading). Sound source, event_time, rate, order, workable: not relevant, use defaults. Findings: e.g., "Head joints not completely filled; visible voids present." Actions: "Remove defective masonry and relay with full mortar bedding" or "Inspect and repoint to fill voids if acceptable per engineer." Rationale: TMS 402/602 requires head joints to be fully filled with mortar; visible voids are a code violation. References: TMS 402/602 (likely section on mortar joint requirements). Confidence: high, 0.95.

So JSON output.

  "rationale": "TMS 402/602 requires all mortar joints to be fully filled solidly. Visible voids in head joints indicate incomplete bedding, a non-compliant and defective condition that reduces load capacity and durability.",
  "references": ["TMS 402/602 Section on mortar joint requirements, likely 6.3 or 8.2 depending on edition"]
}
